basic_details.job_title: Delhi University Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: Delhi University (DU) Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Notification 2026 has been released under Advt. No. PC/RGU/BOT/NMHS/HT/2025-2026/02 on the official website of Delhi University. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the DU Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Recruitment 2026.

qualification.education_qualification: M.Sc. in Botany / Biotechnology with minimum 55% marks, CSIR-UGC NET or NET conducted by any Central Government Departments and their Institutes such as DST, DBT, DAE, DOS, DRDO, MHRD, ICAR, IIT, IISc, IISER etc. (JRF/LS/Assistant Professorship/GATE)
qualification.details: M.Sc. in Botany / Biotechnology with minimum 55% marks. Must have qualified CSIR-UGC NET or NET conducted by any Central Government Departments and their Institutes such as DST, DBT, DAE, DOS, DRDO, MHRD, ICAR, IIT, IISc, IISER etc. (JRF/LS/Assistant Professorship/GATE). Desirable: Background in Plant Taxonomy/Molecular Systematic tools and techniques.

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for Delhi University Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Recruitment 2026 via email should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 28-06-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the Delhi University JRF recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application via email is 28th June 2026. Ensure your application reaches before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the JRF post?
faq.answer: You need an M.Sc. in Botany or Biotechnology with at least 55% marks and a valid CSIR-UGC NET or equivalent qualification.

faq.question: Q3. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q4. How will candidates be selected for the JRF position?
faq.answer: The selection process for the Junior Research Fellow post will be based on an interview.

faq.question: Q5. What is the nature of the job?
faq.answer: The Junior Research Fellow position is on a contractual basis.

faq.question: Q6. Where is the Delhi University located?
faq.answer: Delhi University is located in Delhi, with the pincode 110007.

faq.question: Q7. What is the advertisement number for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The advertisement number is PC/RGU/BOT/NMHS/HT/2025-2026/02.

faq.question: Q8. Are there any age relaxations mentioned?
faq.answer: Age relaxation details are not explicitly mentioned in the provided data; please refer to the official notification.

faq.question: Q9. What is the desirable qualification for the JRF post?
faq.answer: A background in Plant Taxonomy or Molecular Systematic tools and techniques is considered desirable.

faq.question: Q10. How should candidates apply for this position?
faq.answer: Candidates need to apply via email by sending their complete application along with required documents before the last date.
